what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard item that documents the design of the address system: it lists the registered address schemes, which ones resolve versus are deferred, the single dispatcher resolver, parser discipline, versioning, and scope semantics, and it flags a stale resolver error message.
observations: 19 address schemes are registered in contracts/address.py
13 schemes actively resolve and 6 are registered-but-deferred
A single dispatcher, cognition.py:resolve_address, handles one branch per scheme and fails loudly on unknown schemes
Each sub-structured scheme has one declared parser in contracts/address.py
decision:// canonicalizes identity into one state stream
vi-vision:// cascades values by scope rank (global < project < user < session)
The resolver's fail-loud error message is stale: it names 7 resolving schemes while 13 actually resolve
standouts: The address system is described as the unifying spine that lets graphs and chains share one addressed state
The actionable fix is a truth-drift issue: the resolver error message undercounts resolving schemes
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, created/updated timestamps and history
Uses markdown body with a heading and bullet summary
Uses board:// URI as the item's own address
References code paths and line ranges in prose
concerns: The stale error message indicates documentation-to-code drift within the described system
Deferred schemes raise 'not content-resolvable yet', which is a latent TODO without a timeline
Counts and claims are not backed by symbols inside this file
notes: Design note on the address resolver: 19 schemes, one dispatcher, and a stale error message that undercounts the 13 resolving schemes.
questions: Which exact 13 schemes actively resolve and which 6 are deferred?
Is the stale error-message fix already tracked in an issue or task?
What is the content of board://item-228f99c7, the parent this item is part_of?
What prompted the phrase 'more involved than you think, confirmed'?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it documents the address system design and flags a stale resolver error message.
apparent_themes: one addressed state as a unifying spine
single dispatcher with fail-loud extension seam
intention_signals: It is trying to establish the address resolver as the central architectural spine and to prompt a correction so the resolver's error message reflects the actual number of resolving schemes.
novelty: The combination of a cross-scheme version suffix, a scope-rank cascade for vi-vision values, and treating each new capability as one dispatcher branch plus an optional parser.
contribution: A concise inventory of address schemes, resolver architecture, parser/version/scope rules, and a concrete maintenance task to align an error message with current behavior.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item documenting the address system's 19 registered schemes, single dispatcher resolver, one-parser-per-scheme discipline, cross-scheme versioning, identity and scope semantics, and a stale error-message fix.
intention_for_embedding: To record the address resolver design as the unifying spine and flag a documentation-to-code drift fix.
